leave_001:
LEAVE POLICY - COMPREHENSIVE GUIDE

1. ANNUAL LEAVE (PAID TIME OFF)
- Full-time employees: 20 days per calendar year
- Part-time employees: Pro-rata based on hours worked
- Casual employees: 9.3% of hours worked (as per legal requirement)
- Leave year runs from January 1 to December 31
- Unused leave can be carried forward up to 5 days maximum to next year

2. SICK LEAVE
- 10 days per calendar year (paid)
- Can be used for personal illness or caring for immediate family
- Immediate family includes: spouse, children, parents, siblings
- Medical certificate required if 3+ consecutive days taken
- Unused sick leave does NOT carry forward (use-it-or-lose-it policy)

3. PERSONAL LEAVE
- 2 days per calendar year for personal matters
- Examples: house hunting, attending court, attending funeral of extended family
- Requires manager approval with 2 weeks notice
- Cannot be taken consecutively without special circumstances

4. PARENTAL LEAVE
- Maternity Leave: 16 weeks paid + up to 52 weeks unpaid (total 68 weeks)
- Paternity Leave: 2 weeks paid + up to 50 weeks unpaid (total 52 weeks)
- Partner Leave: 2 weeks paid for non-birthing partner
- Can be taken in blocks or as part-time arrangement

5. BEREAVEMENT LEAVE
- Immediate family (spouse, child, parent, sibling): 3 days paid
- Extended family (grandparent, in-law, aunt, uncle, cousin): 1 day paid
- Leave for funeral attendance: Additional 1 day if travel required

6. LEAVE REQUEST PROCEDURE
- Submit minimum 2 weeks in advance to direct manager
- Emergency leave: Notify manager within 24 hours
- Approval based on business requirements and team coverage
- Peak blackout periods: Year-end (Dec 20-31), Diwali week
leave_002:
LEAVE APPLICATION & APPROVAL PROCESS

- Online system: employees.company.com/leave-portal
- Submit request form with dates and reason
- Manager review within 2 business days
- HR approval for policies compliance
- Payment calculated based on leave type
- Email confirmation sent to employee and payroll

LEAVE DURING NOTICE PERIOD
- Can be taken only if manager approves
- Accrued leave must be cleared before separation (or forfeited)
- Unused leave paid out in final settlement (max 5 carried-over days)

LEAVE WITHOUT PAY (LWP)
- Only with HR approval in writing
- Does not count towards service benefits
- Health insurance may be affected
- Common for sabbatical or extended personal matters
benefits_001:
EMPLOYEE BENEFITS PACKAGE

1. HEALTH INSURANCE
- Company covers 100% of base premium for employee
- Family coverage available (spouse and children up to age 25)
- Dental coverage: 50% of procedures, annual limit Rs. 50,000
- Vision coverage: Free annual eye check-up, 50% on glasses/lenses
- Mental health coverage: 10 counseling sessions per year
- No waiting period for outpatient treatment
- Waiting period for inpatient: 30 days

2. LIFE INSURANCE
- Group term life insurance: 3x annual salary
- Accidental death benefit: 5x annual salary
- Disability benefit: 75% of monthly salary
- Beneficiary nomination: Can be updated anytime

3. RETIREMENT BENEFITS
- Provident Fund (PF): 12% of basic salary (employer + employee contribution)
- Employee Pension Scheme (EPS): Automatic enrollment for eligible employees
- Gratuity: 15 days salary per year of service (max 30 months)
- Post-retirement health insurance available

4. WELLNESS PROGRAMS
- Annual health check-up: Free for all employees
- Gym membership: 50% subsidy, max Rs. 5,000/month
- Wellness workshops: Yoga, meditation, nutrition (quarterly)
- Mental health support: Employee Assistance Program (EAP) 24/7
- Annual health camp with free vaccines and screening

5. FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE
- Emergency loan: Up to 3 months salary (no interest)
- Education assistance: Up to Rs. 50,000/year for employee/spouse/children
- Home loan assistance: Facilitation through partner banks (2% discount)
- Vehicle loan assistance: Similar facilitation available
benefits_002:
BENEFITS ELIGIBILITY & ENROLLMENT

EFFECTIVE DATE
- Health insurance: From date of joining
- Life insurance: From date of joining
- Provident Fund: From date of joining
- Gratuity: After 6 months of service
- Retirement benefits: After 1 year of continuous service

ENROLLMENT PROCESS
- HR sends enrollment kit within 3 days of joining
- Complete nomination forms for life insurance and PF
- Submit health insurance declaration
- Update beneficiaries anytime through HR portal

CHANGES & UPDATES
- Family coverage changes: Within 30 days of life event
- Life events: Marriage, child birth, divorce, dependent parent
- Beneficiary changes: Anytime, no documentation needed
- Coverage tier changes: Once per financial year

CLAIM PROCESS
- Health insurance: Cashless at network hospitals, reimbursement within 15 days
- Life insurance: HR processes within 30 days of death certificate
- Disability: Medical examination required, processed within 20 days
compensation_001:
SALARY & COMPENSATION STRUCTURE

1. SALARY COMPONENTS
- Basic Salary: Base component
- Dearness Allowance (DA): 20% of basic (adjusted quarterly)
- House Rent Allowance (HRA): 15-30% of basic based on city
- Conveyance Allowance: Rs. 2,000/month
- Special Allowance: Varies by role and band
- Performance Bonus: 0-100% of basic based on appraisal

2. SALARY STRUCTURE BY LEVEL
- Junior Developer (L1): Rs. 6,00,000 - Rs. 8,00,000 annual
- Senior Developer (L2): Rs. 10,00,000 - Rs. 15,00,000 annual
- Tech Lead (L3): Rs. 18,00,000 - Rs. 25,00,000 annual
- Manager (L4): Rs. 30,00,000 - Rs. 45,00,000 annual
- Director (L5): Rs. 60,00,000 - Rs. 1,00,00,000 annual

3. BONUS & INCENTIVES
- Annual Performance Bonus: Paid in March
- Quarterly Incentive: Sales roles only
- Referral Bonus: Rs. 50,000 - Rs. 2,00,000 per hire
- Patent Bonus: Rs. 25,000 - Rs. 1,00,000

4. SALARY INCREMENTS
- Annual appraisal cycle: January-March
- Increment range: 0% (no increment) to 20%
- Promotion increments: Additional 15-25%
- Effective date: April 1 each year
- Increment review: Based on performance rating
compensation_002:
PAYROLL & PAYMENT DETAILS

- Salary payment: Monthly, 25th of each month
- Mode of payment: Direct bank transfer (NEFT/RTGS)
- Payslip issued: 5th of each month via HR portal
- Tax deduction: As per Income Tax Act, 1961
- PF deduction: 12% of basic
- ESI deduction: 0.75% (if eligible)

PERFORMANCE RATING MATRIX
- Exceeds Expectations (5): 15-20% increment
- Meets Expectations (4): 10-15% increment
- Partially Meets (3): 5-10% increment
- Below Expectations (2): 0-5% increment
- Poor (1): 0% increment, may be put on performance plan

TAX PLANNING
- HRA component: Exempt up to actual rent paid
- LTA: Two journeys per year (air/rail), Rs. 2,00,000 annual limit
- Medical reimbursement: Annual limit Rs. 1,50,000
- Mobile reimbursement: Up to Rs. 2,000/month
work_hours_001:
WORK HOURS & ATTENDANCE POLICY

1. STANDARD WORK HOURS
- Full-time: 40 hours per week
- Monday to Friday: 9:00 AM - 6:00 PM (1 hour lunch break)
- Working days: 5 days per week
- Flexible timings: 8:00 AM - 7:00 PM window available
- No fixed start/end time with manager approval

2. FLEX TIME & CORE HOURS
- Core hours: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM (mandatory presence)
- Flexible arrival: 8:00 AM - 10:00 AM
- Flexible departure: 4:00 PM - 7:00 PM
- Work from home approval: 2 days per week (manager discretion)

3. OVERTIME & COMP-OFF
- Overtime encouraged but not mandatory
- Beyond 50 hours/week: Comp-off at 1:1 ratio
- Comp-off must be taken within 30 days
- No overtime pay (comp-off only)
- Exception: Emergency project work (discussed separately)

4. ATTENDANCE TRACKING
- Biometric system: Manual time tracking acceptable
- Late arrival grace: 5 minutes per day, 30 minutes per week
- No attendance marking on leave days
- Early departure: Requires manager approval
- Absent: Requires manager notification same day

5. HOLIDAYS
- 12 national holidays per calendar year
- 2 optional holidays (employee choice)
- Company-specific holidays: Diwali (3 days), New Year (2 days)
- Regional variations apply for different offices
work_hours_002:
ATTENDANCE & DISCIPLINARY ACTION

ATTENDANCE RULES
- Attendance mandatory minimum: 80% per month
- Excused absences: Leave, public holiday, approved WFH
- Unexcused absence: Can result in disciplinary action
- Serial absenteeism: 3+ days in a month requires HR discussion
- Pattern of absence: May impact performance rating

DISCIPLINARY PROGRESSION
- First offense: Verbal warning
- Second offense (within 6 months): Written warning
- Third offense (within 6 months): Suspension (1 day unpaid)
- Fourth offense: Termination

LEAVE WITHOUT NOTICE
- If absent 3+ consecutive days without notice: Assumed resignation
- HR will attempt to contact employee
- After 5 days: Official termination letter issued
remote_001:
WORK FROM HOME (WFH) POLICY

1. WFH ELIGIBILITY
- Tenure: Minimum 3 months in organization
- Performance rating: Must be "Meets Expectations" or higher
- Role: Must not require in-person presence (e.g., no client meetings)
- Manager approval: Required for each request

2. WFH ALLOWANCE
- Frequency: Up to 2 days per week
- Contingent days: Additional 2 days for urgent work (manager approval)
- COVID-related: 100% WFH available on request
- Frequency change: Requires 2 weeks notice

3. WFH REQUIREMENTS
- Must maintain core hours: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM
- Video calls: Expected during meetings
- Slack/chat: Active and responsive
- Work location: Safe and secure (no public Wi-Fi)
- Internet requirement: Broadband minimum 10 Mbps

4. WFH RESTRICTIONS
- Client-facing roles: Limited to 1 day per week
- New team members: First 3 months office-only
- During probation: Only after approval
- Project milestones: May be restricted during critical periods

5. INTERNET REIMBURSEMENT
- WFH internet subsidy: Rs. 1,000/month
- Valid bill required (in employee's name or home)
- Reimbursement processed monthly
remote_002:
HYBRID & INTERNATIONAL WFH

HYBRID ARRANGEMENTS
- Possible for long-term arrangements
- Requires Director-level approval
- Split: E.g., 3 days office, 2 days WFH
- Review period: Quarterly
- Performance impact: Tracked and discussed

INTERNATIONAL WFH
- Max 30 days per calendar year
- Requires HR and Manager approval
- Tax implications: Employee responsibility
- Timezone consideration: Can work across timezone if needed
- Must maintain 80% of core hours in company timezone

EQUIPMENT & SETUP
- Laptop: Company provided
- Monitor/keyboard: Available for pick-up (reimbursable up to Rs. 20,000)
- Chair: Employee responsibility or reimbursement available
- Desk: Employee responsibility
performance_001:
PERFORMANCE MANAGEMENT & APPRAISAL

1. APPRAISAL CYCLE
- Frequency: Annual (January-March)
- 360-degree feedback: From manager, peers (3-4), and self
- Rating scale: 1 (Poor) to 5 (Exceeds Expectations)
- Consistency check: Calibration meeting with senior management
- Discussion: One-on-one with manager before finalization

2. APPRAISAL CRITERIA
- Technical skills: 30%
- Project delivery: 30%
- Soft skills & teamwork: 20%
- Innovation & learning: 10%
- Attendance & punctuality: 10%

3. RATING DISTRIBUTION
- Exceeds Expectations (5): Top 10%
- Meets Expectations (4): 60-70%
- Partially Meets (3): 15-20%
- Below Expectations (2): 5-10%
- Poor (1): 0-5%

4. PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN (PIP)
- Duration: 3 months
- Review checkpoint: Monthly with manager
- Success criteria: Clearly defined at start
- Support provided: Training, mentoring, additional resources
- Outcome: Promotion to regular performance or termination

5. PROMOTION CRITERIA
- Tenure: Minimum 18 months in current role
- Rating: Exceeds Expectations (5) for 2 consecutive years
- Skills: Competency in next role demonstrated
- Recommendation: From direct manager and senior stakeholders
- Board approval: For senior positions
performance_002:
LEARNING & DEVELOPMENT

TRAINING BUDGET
- Annual L&D budget: Rs. 30,000 per employee
- Technical courses: Fully covered
- Soft skills: 50% covered
- MBA/higher education: Up to Rs. 1,00,000 (with bond)

LEARNING PROGRAMS
- Internal training: Weekly tech talks and workshops
- External courses: LinkedIn Learning, Coursera, Udemy
- Certification support: AWS, Google Cloud, Azure (full reimbursement)
- Conference attendance: Once per year (manager approval)

SKILL DEVELOPMENT
- New technology adoption: 1 week dedicated learning time per quarter
- Leadership training: For managers and above
- Communication workshop: Recommended for all levels
- Cross-functional project: Encouraged for skill diversification

KNOWLEDGE SHARING
- Internal sessions: Employee encouraged to present learnings
- Blog/documentation: Recognition for knowledge sharing
- Mentor program: Formal mentoring available
- Pair programming: Encouraged with senior team members
conduct_001:
CODE OF CONDUCT & WORKPLACE BEHAVIOR

1. GENERAL CONDUCT
- Respectful workplace: Zero tolerance for harassment or discrimination
- Professional communication: Both in-person and written
- Confidentiality: NDA binding for all confidential information
- Conflict of interest: Must be disclosed to HR
- Social media: Represent company positively

2. PROHIBITED CONDUCT
- Harassment: Sexual, verbal, physical (any form)
- Discrimination: Based on gender, religion, caste, disability, sexual orientation
- Violence: Physical assault or threatening behavior
- Theft: Company or personal property
- Fraud: Falsifying records, expense claims
- Intoxication: Reporting to work under influence
- Unauthorized access: Company systems or data

3. DISCIPLINARY ACTION
- Verbal warning: First minor offense
- Written warning: Repeated offense or serious conduct
- Suspension: 1-3 days for major violations
- Termination: Gross misconduct (no notice period)
- Legal action: For criminal conduct

4. WORKPLACE SAFETY
- Fire safety: Emergency drill quarterly
- First aid: Training available
- Ergonomics: Workstation setup guidance
- Mental health: Support and counseling available
- Violence prevention: Reporting mechanism in place

5. DRESS CODE
- Smart casual: Office environment
- Jeans: Allowed on Friday (casual day)
- T-shirts with logo: Allowed
- Inappropriate: Torn clothes, religious/political symbols
- Exceptions: Medical reasons with HR approval
safety_001:
WORKPLACE SAFETY & SECURITY POLICY

1. BUILDING ACCESS
- ID card: Mandatory for all employees
- Visitor log: All visitors must sign in
- After-hours access: Manager approval required
- Parking: Assigned spots available (first-come, first-served)
- Emergency exits: Clearly marked, no obstruction

2. DATA SECURITY
- Password policy: Minimum 12 characters, change quarterly
- Two-factor authentication: Mandatory for all accounts
- Laptop encryption: All devices must be encrypted
- Public Wi-Fi: VPN required for remote work
- Confidential documents: Must be locked/secured

3. INCIDENT REPORTING
- Accident: Report to manager within 24 hours
- Security breach: Report to IT immediately
- Harassment/discrimination: Report to HR or Ethics hotline
- Near-miss: Also report for preventive action
- Anonymous reporting: Available through ethics hotline

4. EMERGENCY PROCEDURES
- Fire alarm: Evacuate immediately to assembly point
- Medical emergency: Call security/HR
- Bomb threat: Follow evacuation procedure
- Active threat: Shelter-in-place procedure
- All-clear: Only when management confirms
termination_001:
SEPARATION & TERMINATION POLICY

1. RESIGNATION PROCESS
- Notice period: 2 months for regular staff, 1 month for probation
- Resignation letter: In writing to manager and HR
- Acceptance: By HR (automatic if 2 months passed)
- During notice: Continue regular duties
- Full-and-final: Settlement within 15 days of exit

2. RESIGNATION SETTLEMENT
- Final salary: Paid on last working day
- Accrued leave: Paid at full value
- Gratuity: Calculated and paid
- Bonus: If applicable, pro-rata calculation
- Benefits: Health insurance ends on exit date

3. TERMINATION BY COMPANY
- Performance: After PIP failure (written notice + severance)
- Conduct: Immediate for gross misconduct (notice + severance per policy)
- Retrenchment: 1 month notice + 3 months severance
- Redundancy: 1 month notice + salary continuation + job assistance

4. EXIT FORMALITIES
- Equipment return: Laptop, ID, keys, etc.
- Accounts: All pending expenses reimbursed
- Knowledge transfer: 2-week handover period
- Reference letter: Provided to all
- Alumni network: Invited to join

5. SEVERANCE PAY
- Termination for conduct: 0 severance
- Termination for performance (PIP): 1 month salary
- Retrenchment: 3 months salary
- Redundancy: 6 months salary
- Golden handshake: Negotiable for senior positions
